title: InspectorReadV1 trait + inspector_read crate (compile-time read-only enforcement)
goal: Per spec §6.5.2 (AC-INSPECTOR-PLANE-READONLY) introduce the `InspectorReadV1` Rust trait with NO `&mut self` methods, living in a new `inspector_read` crate that does NOT depend on the write-side crate; a workspace-level deny-lint blocks any reverse dependency edge so mutation cannot leak into the inspector path at compile time.
expected_diff_shape: New crate src/backend/handshake_core/src/inspector_read/{mod.rs,trait_def.rs,impls.rs}; workspace Cargo.toml deny-lint entry preventing inspector_read from depending on kernel write-side modules; tests prove the trait surface has zero mutable methods.
unblock_conditions: No prerequisites; foundation MT for A.3.
implementation_notes: Per spec §6.5.2 the InspectorReadV1 trait surface is the compile-time guarantee that the inspector cannot mutate. Trait shape: `pub trait InspectorReadV1 { fn list_sessions(&self) -> Vec<SessionSummary>; fn session_state(&self, id: SessionId) -> Option<SessionStateRead>; fn event_ledger_tail(&self, n: usize) -> Vec<EventLedgerRow>; fn process_ledger_active(&self) -> Vec<ProcessRow>; fn workspace_state_read(&self, ws_id: WorkspaceId) -> Option<WorkspaceStateRead>; fn trace_projection(&self, session_id: SessionId) -> Option<TraceProjection>; fn loaded_models(&self) -> Vec<ModelLoadedRow>; }`. NO `&mut self`, NO interior-mutability inside `&self` methods (use Arc<RwLock> only for read paths). All return types are owned/clones to prevent borrow-leak. impls.rs provides one concrete impl that takes immutable references to the read-only views of KERNEL-001 (EventLedger), KERNEL-002 (CRDT workspace), MT-007 (ProcessLedger), and cluster C (ModelRuntime — placeholder when not yet landed). Workspace Cargo.toml: per [GLOBAL-GOVARTIFACTS] the inspector_read module must not depend on write-side modules. Since this MT lives inside the same Cargo crate as the write side, enforce the boundary via a `cargo-deny` (or a custom workspace lint) rule: forbid `use crate::kernel::write::*` from `inspector_read` files. Add cargo-deny config to deny.toml with a `[bans]` section listing forbidden source paths. Alternative if cargo-deny path-based bans are not supported: a grep-based check in .GOV/roles_shared/checks/inspector-read-isolation.mjs that ripgreps for forbidden imports and exits 2 on hit. Tests: assert by reflection (compile-fail trick) that adding `&mut self` to a trait method fails compilation; assert the trait can be implemented and called from a `Arc<dyn InspectorReadV1>` context.
risk_if_missed: Without the read-only trait, a future inspector endpoint could accidentally take &mut self and silently expose a mutation path; spec §6.5 single-mutation-path guarantee is breached.
validator_focus: Trait surface has ZERO &mut self methods; cargo-deny or grep check enforces no reverse dependency to write-side; compile-fail test confirms the boundary is enforced at compile time.

minimum_controls: Compile-fail test enforces zero &mut self in trait surface.
cargo-deny or grep check enforces no reverse dependency to write-side.
All return types are owned (no borrowed references that could leak mutation context).
